betCard.title: Round 6 Charity Bet
why: Cowboys come in off back-to-back wins including a 32-0 result.
Walsh being out changes Brisbane's attacking shape.
The price is longer than the form line suggests.
risks: Broncos have a strong home and head-to-head record in this matchup.
The attached snapshot mentions Reynolds uncertainty, which should be re-checked.
Derby games can flip quickly on field position and discipline.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 is pending.

watchlist: Panthers are the benchmark until someone makes them look mortal.
Tigers and Knights both sitting 4-1 gives the round real intrigue.
Dragons and Raiders are the crisis clubs in this snapshot.
Broncos team news matters more than historical narrative this week.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 5 wrapped, Round 6 opened
lines: The original briefing captures a confident Round 5 recap with the Knights bet landing and six correct tips from eight.
The Panthers, Tigers, Knights, and Rabbitohs dominate the narrative as the early movers in this snapshot.
The copy is punchy and fun, but it needs a safer update workflow because almost every line is time-sensitive sports data.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Cowboys were selected as the Round 6 value play
lines: The attached app leans on form over fortress logic: Cowboys trending up, Broncos weakened by key injuries.
That call is still presented here, but the new version explicitly labels it as a pending verification pick.
The upgraded layout separates reasons to back the pick from reasons to pause, which makes staking decisions calmer and clearer.

lines: Fri 10 Apr: Dragons vs Sea Eagles

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who looked hot, who looked broken
lines: Hot: Panthers, Knights, Tigers, Rabbitohs, Cowboys.
The improved app keeps this summary but moves it into a reusable structured section instead of raw inline styling rules.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before publishing
lines: Broncos injuries, especially Walsh and Reynolds, materially affect the betting logic.
Any ladder or streak claim should be treated as snapshot data, not a live feed.
The improved dashboard now foregrounds these caveats instead of hiding them inside the body copy.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
